title: What is the difference between BeanFactory and ApplicationContext in Spring?

answers.junior: BeanFactory is a basic container for managing beans. ApplicationContext is more advanced and provides extra features like events and internationalization.
answers.middle: BeanFactory manages beans and dependencies. ApplicationContext adds features like event handling, resource loading, and eager bean initialization. Use ApplicationContext for most applications.
answers.senior: ApplicationContext extends BeanFactory, offering AOP, transaction management, and integration with Spring Boot. BeanFactory is for lightweight or legacy cases. ApplicationContext is preferred for modern apps.
answers.tricky: ApplicationContext eagerly initializes singleton beans and supports advanced features. BeanFactory is lazy and lacks event handling. Using BeanFactory can miss out on automatic wiring and cause subtle bugs.
